title: Transduction of i3Neurons Support Protocol
author: Michael S. Fernandopulle
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5wvg7e6
materials: Agitate plate to evenly distribute transfection solution.

Optional: Replace medium on HEK cells with 1 mL to 2 mL fresh HEK medium, to be collected the following day. This process can be repeated until Day 6 to collect more virus. Be aware that viral titer decreases with each collection. Lentiviral preps can also be concentrated using various commercially available reagents (e.g., Clontech, cat. no. 631231 ).
Immediately flick the tube several times to mix.

steps.title: Coat a 6-well dish with PLO...
steps.instruction: Coat a 6-well dish with PLO (see Basic Protocol 6 ).

steps.title: Check cells for confluency under an inverted microscope
steps.instruction: Check cells for confluency under an inverted microscope. Cells should be > 95 % confluent for maximal viral production efficiency If necessary, wait a day to transfect so that the cells are nearly confluent.

steps.title: Plate 2
steps.instruction: Plate 2.5 × 10 6 Lenti-X HEK cells in 6-well dish well in 1.5 mL DMEM/F12 containing 10 % FBS (HEK medium).

steps.title: Warm two tubes with 150 µL...
steps.instruction: Warm two tubes with 150 µL Opti-MEM each to Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.6 µg psPAX, 0.6 µg pMD302, 0.2 µg pAdvantage, 2.4 µg pLEX viral vector, and 10 µL P3000 reagent to tube A.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.87 µL Lipofectamine 3000 to tube B.

steps.title: Incubate tubes at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate tubes at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Add the contents of tube A...
steps.instruction: Add the contents of tube A into tube B with a P200.

steps.title: Incubate tube at Room temperature for 00:20:00 to 00:40:00
steps.instruction: Incubate tube at Room temperature for 00:20:00 to 00:40:00 .

steps.title: Add contents of tube dropwise to...
steps.instruction: Add contents of tube dropwise to to the medium of Lenti-X cells with a P1000.

steps.title: Replace medium on Lenti-X HEK cells...
steps.instruction: Replace medium on Lenti-X HEK cells with 3 mL of warm, fresh HEK medium supplemented with 6 µL of 500× ViralBoost reagent.

steps.title: Check cells with a fluorescent microscope...
steps.instruction: Check cells with a fluorescent microscope to ensure expression of any fluorescent proteins in the viral vector.

steps.title: Check cells under inverted microscope for...
steps.instruction: Check cells under inverted microscope for multinucleated morphology (indicating viral production) and fluorescent protein production.

steps.title: Filter to purify virus from floating HEK cells
steps.instruction: Filter to purify virus from floating HEK cells. Instead of filtering, medium may be centrifuged at > for 00:10:00 at 4 °C to remove cells/debris, followed by transferring the supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Either use immediately or freeze aliquots at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Either use immediately or freeze aliquots at -80 °C .
